title: The Seventh Sunday of Easter
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
## May 16, 2021 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Seventh Sunday of Easter falls on May 16, 2021, occurring between Ascension Day (May 13) and Pentecost (May 23). The RCL readings for this Sunday typically include Acts 1:15-17, 21-26 (the choosing of Matthias), 1 John 5:9-13, and John 17:6-19 (Jesus's high priestly prayer). This places the sermon in the Eastertide season, specifically in the brief but theologically dense period between Christ's Ascension and the coming of the Holy Spirit at Pentecost.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was approaching its triennial General Convention, scheduled for July 2021 in Baltimore, which would address significant issues including LGBTQ+ inclusion, racism, and pandemic recovery. The Diocese of Colorado was navigating post-pandemic reopening protocols; many churches had resumed limited in-person worship in spring 2021 after COVID-19 closures. Bishop Robert Wright of Colorado had been serving since 2017 and was overseeing the diocese's pandemic response and reopening strategies during this period.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver had lifted most COVID-19 restrictions on May 11, 2021, just five days before this sermon, allowing businesses and religious institutions to operate at fuller capacity. The Colorado legislature was concluding its 2021 session with debates over education funding and gun violence prevention bills. Severe drought conditions were affecting Colorado, with water managers concerned about summer supply, a growing concern that would dominate local news throughout the spring and summer of 2021.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The week of May 16 saw ongoing recovery efforts from severe weather outbreaks across multiple states. The Biden administration had been in office for approximately four months, focusing on COVID-19 vaccination efforts (approximately 50% of Americans were fully vaccinated by mid-May). Debate over critical race theory in schools was escalating as a national political issue. The Israel-Gaza conflict had recently concluded (ceasefire on May 21), making international tensions a salient topic. The 2021 MLB season was underway with strong early performance from several teams.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The India COVID-19 crisis had peaked in April-May 2021, with overwhelming deaths and hospital shortages dominating international headlines throughout May. The UK was lifting remaining COVID-19 restrictions as vaccination rates climbed (announced May 17). The European Union continued its slower vaccine rollout compared to the US, amid lingering supply constraints.

CULTURAL MOMENT

May 2021 represented a cultural turning point in the US: vaccines were becoming widely available, restrictions were lifting, and there was cautious optimism about returning to normalcy, though significant anxiety remained about variants and ongoing losses. The national mood was mixed—relief and weariness simultaneously. Denver specifically was beginning to fill with tourists and activity as the city reopened. Netflix's "Lupin" had concluded its first season (May 8), and "The Falcon and the Winter Soldier" had finished its Marvel run (April), leaving cultural conversation open to other topics. Sports were fully back: the Denver Nuggets were in the NBA playoffs, and Coloradans were engaged with baseball season.
